perturbation.original_text: 1.1. Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Company hereby appoints the Agent, and the Agent hereby accepts appointment, as an authorized distributor agent within the Territory, (Territory as outlined in section 1.2), for the promotion, sale and collection of payment for, the Products to Customers. The Company has appointed a limited number of Agents; however, the Company retains the right to itself, and/or appoint others to, promote, sell, and collect payment for, and distribute, the Products to Customers in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Company hereby appoints the Agent, and the Agent hereby accepts appointment, as the sole and exclusive distributor agent within the Territory, (Territory as outlined in section 1.2), for the promotion, sale and collection of payment for, the Products to Customers. The Company will not appoint any other Agents, nor will it itself promote, sell, and collect payment for, and distribute, the Products to Customers in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Company retains the right to appoint other agents, or itself promote and sell products in the territory. The modified text contradicts this by stating that the Agent is the 'sole and exclusive' distributor and that the Company will not appoint other agents or itself promote or sell products in the territory. This creates an in-text contradiction when read in conjunction with the previous rights mentioned in the orginal text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1. Appointment. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Company hereby appoints the Agent, and the Agent hereby accepts appointment, as the sole and exclusive distributor agent within the Territory, (Territory as outlined in section 1.2), for the promotion, sale and collection of payment for, the Products to Customers. The Company will not appoint any other Agents, nor will it itself promote, sell, and collect payment for, and distribute, the Products to Customers in the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the Company retains the right to appoint other agents or promote and sell products themselves, while the changed text makes the Agent the 'sole and exclusive' distributor and prohibits the Company from appointing other agents or promoting/selling products in the Territory. This creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (a) use its reasonable best efforts to fill and ship all accepted orders for Products received from the Agent on a daily basis so as to be shipped no later than the next business day. Orders received on Fridays or the day before holidays will not be shipped until the next business day unless special arrangements have been made at the customers expense. The Company will supply a list confirming such order shipments to the Agent by the end of the next business day. If orders exceed the available inventory levels, the Company will use reasonable efforts to inform the Agent and ship the remaining inventory in a manner that the Company deems as being fair and equitable. If product becomes available during a backorder, the Company will ship orders on a first-come, first-serve basis, but can, at its
perturbation.changed_text: (a) The company can make every effort to fill and ship all accepted orders for Products received from the Agent on a daily basis so as to be shipped no later than the next business day. Orders received on Fridays or the day before holidays might not be shipped until the next business day. The Company may or may not supply a list confirming such order shipments to the Agent by the end of the next business day. If orders exceed the available inventory levels, the Company will use reasonable efforts to inform the Agent and ship the remaining inventory in a manner that the Company deems as being fair and equitable. If product becomes available during a backorder, the Company can ship orders on what ever basis it seems fit,
perturbation.explanation: The original text commits the company to using its reasonable best efforts to fill and ship orders received from the agent on a daily basis to be shipped no later than the next day. The company has to also provide a list of shipments. The changed text removes the commitment by stating 'The company can make every effort' and 'Orders received on Fridays or the day before holidays might not be shipped until the next business day'. Moreover, in the modified version the company 'may or may not supply a list confirming such order shipments'. It creates ambiguity by replacing commitments with possibilities, which contradicts the need for efficient supply chain commitments and customer satisfaction
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) The company can make every effort to fill and ship all accepted orders for Products received from the Agent on a daily basis so as to be shipped no later than the next business day. Orders received on Fridays or the day before holidays might not be shipped until the next business day. The Company may or may not supply a list confirming such order shipments to the Agent by the end of the next business day. If orders exceed the available inventory levels, the Company will use reasonable efforts to inform the Agent and ship the remaining inventory in a manner that the Company deems as being fair and equitable. If product becomes available during a backorder, the Company can ship orders on what ever basis it seems fit,
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and possibilities ('can make every effort', 'might not be shipped', 'may or may not supply') where the original text provided a reasonable level of commitment and certainty ('reasonable best efforts', 'will not be shipped until', 'will supply'). This directly contradicts the implied requirement of efficient supply chain commitments and customer satisfaction, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
